title: Fix x-assign unique when index out-of-range of an unpacked struct array

features.name: unpacked struct array with ternary conditional indexing
features.description: Code should include an unpacked struct array accessed via ternary conditional where index may exceed array bounds, used with --x-assign unique

features.name: struct member access in ternary true branch
features.description: Code should include a struct member access placed outside a ternary operator where the true branch is an unpacked array element, causing type mismatch between struct and scalar in false branch

features.name: pointer width exceeding array size
features.description: Code should include a pointer/index with bit width based on $clog2 of array size, which can represent values equal to or exceeding the array size when used as index
